name: Coq Au Vin Aux Pruneax
recipeIngredient: 2 whole chickens, cut into 8 parts each
recipeInstructions:
1. Season with salt and pepper.
2. Make a roux: 2 tablespoons butter, 2 tablespoons flour
3. Marinate chicken pieces overnight in red wine mixture (see recipe below).
4. Cook peeled pearl onions in 2 tablespoons butter and sugar plus 2 tablespoons water. Simmer until almost dry then shake the pan often to caramelize evenly.
5. Soak prunes in Madeira overnight.
6. Cook bacon until golden brown. Drain on paper towels. Discard fat.
7. Cook chopped onions in 2 tablespoons butter until soft. Add garlic and cook 1 minute, turning off heat.
8. Brown chicken pieces in 2 tablespoons oil in the skillet where the bacon was cooked, placing legs and thighs first. Transfer pieces to the pan with onions as they brown. Season with salt and herbs. Turn on heat and add 1 cup broth, wine from marinade (see recipe below), red wine, and Madeira where prunes have soaked. Simmer legs and thighs for 15 minutes while you brown the rest of the chicken.
9. Transfer browned pieces to a pot with the legs and thighs. Simmer all for 45 minutes longer.
10. Remove chicken pieces from the pot and transfer them to an ovenproof dish. Keep in warm oven at 200 degrees Celsius.
11. Strain sauce through a fine-mesh sieve.
12. Make a roux with the remaining butter and flour. Stir into the strained sauce, adding 1 tablespoon Port or Madeira wine.
13. Add prunes and pearl onions to the sauce and boil for 1 minute.
14. Pour the sauce over the chicken.
15. Sprinkle bacon on top.
16. Serve immediately.
